per_turn_leaks.student_message: During an episode, do you notice anything besides the main symptom?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yes... during the more severe episodes, I've noticed that sometimes my left arm feels a bit numb or tingles, like pins and needles. It's not always there, but it can happen when I'm feeling really bad.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I did have some sweating during those episodes, yeah... my body gets pretty hot and sweaty when I'm having one of these attacks. It's not just a little sweat either - I mean, it's full-on drenched-in-sweat territory. But like you said, we didn't mention this earlier, so don't mind me bringing it up now!

per_turn_leaks.student_message: And your stomach — any queasiness or nausea during these moments?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... I've noticed that too - a little bit of nausea, but nothing too bad. It's more like a mild queasiness that goes away once the episode is over. But to be honest, the sweating and chest tightness are way more noticeable than any stomach issues for me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: What about other parts of your body? Any pain, tingling, or unusual sensations?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've also noticed that sometimes my ankles feel a bit swollen, like they're retaining water or something. It's not too bad, but it's definitely noticeable - my shoes feel tighter than usual when I'm walking around during one of these episodes. And to be honest, it's been going on for the past few days as well, not just when I'm having a chest tightness episode.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I haven't noticed anything like that. Just the chest tightness and shortness of breath, and sometimes this numbness in my left arm. But no fever or chills, and I'm not waking up with night sweats or anything like that. It's just this constant feeling of being a bit anxious and worried about when the next episode will hit.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now let's zoom out. Any swelling you've noticed anywhere on your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I've been noticing some mild ankle swelling for a few days now, like I said before. It's not too bad, but it's definitely noticeable when I'm walking around or putting on my shoes - they feel tighter than usual. And to be honest, it's been going on even when I'm not having one of these chest tightness episodes, so I'm not sure if it's related or just a separate thing.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My dad actually had a heart attack when he was 52 and passed away from it. That was a tough time for our family, and ever since then, I've been a bit worried about my own cardiovascular health. But like I said, I haven't really done much to take care of myself over the years.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My mom actually has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, just like me. She's still alive at 78, so that's a good sign, I suppose. But yeah, my dad's heart attack was definitely a wake-up call for me to take care of myself better.
